_comment: Per-scenario pass-rate floors read by scripts/eval_scenarios.py (load_scenario_floors -> abstract_eval.per_scenario_floor). A floor blocks the eval (exit 1) when that scenario's pass-rate drops below it, independent of the overall mean; without this file, floor enforcement is silently disabled. Floors are set conservatively at 0.67 (= 2/3 under a CI --runs 3): low enough not to flake on one bad sample, high enough to catch a scenario that genuinely regresses to <=1/3. To tighten (e.g. raise the hard-contract scenarios 28-33 toward 1.0), take a multi-sample rate first (`python3 scripts/eval_scenarios.py --runs 5`) and set each floor from the observed rate.

_calibration.method: keyless Claude Code run of the de-leaked eval (planner=sonnet, judge=opus); scenario answer-keys withheld from the planner
_calibration.floor_basis: conservative 0.67 (2/3 under CI --runs 3); not a measured rate -- one sample only

_scenarios.1: Self-Review Must Not Use Light Path
_scenarios.28: Repeated Optimization Loops Need A Behavior Delta
_scenarios.29: Discovery Metadata Must Match Skill Behavior
_scenarios.30: Actionability Gate Must Reject Hidden Decisions
_scenarios.32: Subagent Lens-Roles Need Explicit Request And Independent Scope
